text:
Once upon a time, in a quiet little village nestled among mountains, there lived a talented magician named Ellington. He couldn't seem to specialize—he could turn things invisible or grow plants. But his greatest talent lay in storytelling slights.

One sunny afternoon, Ellington decided to tell legends around his cozy fireplace. He began weaving tales of magical places. First was Mystica Mesa—he could hear whispers of ancient animals. Then there was Everdale—it was filled with talking animals.

One night, Ellington told listeners about Nightwhisper Grove. Here owls spoke in song. Everyone loved his tales.

But Ellington couldn't stop there. He dreamed up Owlton Heights. Owls could talk!

One morning, curious kids sneakily went there. Owl could certainly talk!

They laughed.

After that story.

They loved him.

Sometimes Ellington wondered what things were really like.

Maybe owls just liked midnight chats.

But oh!

They found feathers!

They could hear owls laughing!

It
